title: Analyze a configured event group through Biblicus

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
As an operator, I want Caducus to analyze a configured event group through Biblicus, so that recurring operational patterns and anomalies are surfaced without Caducus reimplementing the analysis engine.

Feature: Biblicus-backed event analysis
  Scenario: Analyze one configured event group
    Given canonical event records exist for a configured group_id
    And the Caducus configuration contains a biblicus subtree for reinforcement memory settings
    When I run the Caducus analyze command for that group
    Then Caducus passes the Biblicus configuration through without duplicating its schema
    And Caducus invokes Biblicus reinforcement memory analysis for that group
    And Caducus persists the resulting analysis outputs

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Contract-based Gherkin (see docs/caducus-biblicus-contract.md):

  Scenario: Map canonical events to TimestampedText and run analysis
    Given canonical event records exist with id, timestamp, group_id, summary_text, and metadata
    And the biblicus config subtree specifies reinforcement_memory data_dir and vector_store
    When I run the Caducus analyze command for that group_id
    Then Caducus maps each event to TimestampedText using event.id, group_id, timestamp, summary_text, metadata
    And Caducus invokes Biblicus ReinforcementMemory.ingest then analyze(group_id=...)
    And Caducus exposes AnalysisResult as stable CLI output (label, memory_tier, lifecycle_tier, member_count, root_cause)

  Scenario: One group per analyze run
    Given multiple group_ids have collected events
    When I run the Caducus analyze command for one group_id
    Then Caducus runs analysis only for that group_id
    And Caducus does not mix vector store state with other groups in the same run

  Scenario: group_id default from source and logical id
    Given a collector without an explicit group_id override
    When events are collected
    Then each event has group_id set to source_type:logical_id
    And analysis can be run for that group_id

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Terminology update: canonical event field is , not . Contract-aligned mapping is now event.id, group_id, timestamp, text, metadata -> TimestampedText.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Contract terminology correction: canonical event field is text, not summary_text.

Updated mapping:
- event.id -> TimestampedText.id
- event.group_id -> TimestampedText.group_id
- event.timestamp -> TimestampedText.timestamp
- event.text -> TimestampedText.text
- event.metadata -> TimestampedText.metadata
